title: Atomic execution unit for object storage
abstract: A computer-implemented method for translating file system operations to object store operations may include necessary steps to receive a plurality of file system operations for operating files in a file system; determine corresponding objects and object store operations in an object store for the files and the file system operations; determine an order of the object store operations based on time of the file system operations received in the file system; determine dependency of the object store operations, and assign the object store operations to a first queue based on the order and dependency; determine priority of the object store operations, and transfer an entry containing an object store operation with the priority from the first queue to a second queue; and execute the object store operations in parallel and asynchronously based on organization of the object store operations in the first and second queues.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system for efficient execution of a plurality of obj ect store operations as one atomic operation, the system comprising: an obj ect storage system configured to store data as objects, the obj ect storage system having an input and an output, the input of the object storage system coupled to receive commands for execution and data for storage in the obj ect storage system, the output of the object storage system for providing data; and an atomic execution unit configured to execute the plurality of obj ect store operations as the one atomic operation, the atomic execution unit having a first input, a second input, a first output and a second output, the first input and the first output coupled to the object storage system for access and storage of the objects, the second input of the atomic execution unit to receive one or more operations on the obj ects in the object storage system from concurrent users, the second output coupled to provide data from the obj ect storage. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the atomic execution unit comprises a file interface module configured to receive file system operations and decouple them from perfect sync object store operations and an object operation manager configured to organize these object store operations for atomic and efficient operation, the file interface module coupled for communication with the obj ect operation manager and a network, the object operation manager coupled for communication with the object storage system. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the obj ect operation manager includes an operations queue for storing sequences of object store operations. 4. The system of claim 3, wherein an obj ect store operation involving a particular object identifier is stored as part of a same entry in the operations queue and part of a same dependency tree. 5. The system of claim 2, wherein the obj ect operation manager includes a hash table to correlate each entry of an operations queue with a respective obj ect identifier. 6. The system of claim 2, wherein the object operation manager includes a dependency action manager for determining dependency based on objects affected by multiple object store operations. 7. The system of claim 2, wherein the object operation manager includes a high priority queue and a lower priority queue to allow parallel processing of entries from the high priority queue then entries from the lower priority queue, and to allow asynchronous processing of a dependency tree in the high priority queue and the lower priority queue. 8. A computer-implemented method comprising: receiving a plurality of file system operations for operating files in a file system; determining corresponding objects in an object store for the operating files and corresponding object store operations for the plurality of the file system operations; determining an order of the object store operations based on a time when the plurality of the file system operations were received in the file system; determining dependency of the object store operations based on objects affected by the object store operations, and assigning the object store operations to a first queue based on the order and the dependency; determining priority of the object store operations based on types of object store operations, and transferring an entry with an object store operation and a priority from the first queue to a second queue; and executing the object store operations in parallel and asynchronously based on organization of the object store operations in the first and second queues. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein determining dependency of the object store operations based on objects affected by the object store operations comprises: determining an object affected by a first object store operation; determining whether the object is affected by a second object store operation; and in response to determining that the object is affected by the second object store operation, determining an existence of dependency between the first and second object store operations. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein assigning object store operations to the first queue based on the order and dependency comprises: determining unassigned obj ect store operations in the corresponding object store operations; and ranking the unassigned object store operations in a list according to the order of the corresponding obj ect store operations. 1 1. The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein assigning obj ect store operations to the first queue based on the order and dependency further comprises: selecting a first-ranked obj ect store operation in the list; identifying an object affected by the first-ranked obj ect store operation; determining whether the identified obj ect already has an existing obj ect store operations; in response to determining that the identified obj ect already has existing object store operations, determining whether the identified object is detected in two or more existent entries; in response to determining that the identified obj ect is detected in the two or more existent entries, merging the two or more existent entries into a new dependency tree and adding the merged dependency tree as an entry to the first queue; adding the first-ranked object store operation to the merged dependency tree; and updating the list of the unassigned obj ect store operations by removing the first- ranked obj ect store operation from the list. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein executing the obj ect store operations in parallel and asynchronously based on organization of the obj ect store operations in the first and second queues comprises: determining whether there are a plurality of entries in the second queue; and in response to determining that there is an entry in the second queue, executing entries in the second queue in parallel. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 12, wherein executing the obj ect store operations in parallel and asynchronously based on organization of the obj ect store operations in the first and second queues comprises: 

 determining whether an entry in the first queue has a first dependency tree in the second queue; and responsive to the entry in the first queue having the first dependency tree in the second queue, sequentially executing obj ect store operations in the first dependency tree in a general left-to-right order. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein determining priority of the obj ect store operations based on types of object store operations, and transferring an entry with an object store operation with a priority from the first queue to a second queue comprises: determining an operation type for an obj ect store operation; determining whether the operation type has a high priority; in response to determining that the operation type has the high priority, assigning the object store operation the priority; determining whether an entry in the first queue includes an obj ect store operation with the priority; and in response to determining that the entry in the first queue includes the obj ect store operation with the priority, transferring the entry into the second queue. 15. A system comprising: means for receiving a plurality of file system operations for operating files in a file system; means for determining corresponding objects in an obj ect store for the operating files and corresponding object store operations for the plurality of the file system operations; means for determining an order of the obj ect store operations based on a time when the plurality of the file system operations were received in the file system; means for determining dependency of the obj ect store operations based on objects affected by the object store operations, and assigning the obj ect store operations to a first queue based on the order and the dependency; means for determining priority of the obj ect store operations based on types of obj ect store operations, and transferring an entry with an object store operation and a priority from the first queue to a second queue; and 

 means for executing the object store operations in parallel and asynchronously based on organization of the obj ect store operations in the first and second queues. 16. The system of claim 15 further comprising: means for determining an obj ect affected by a first obj ect store operation; means for determining whether the object is affected by a second object store operation; and means for determining an existence of dependency between the first and second object store operations, in response to determining that the object is affected by the second object store operation. 17. The system of claim 15 further comprising: means for determining unassigned obj ect store operations in the corresponding object store operations; and means for ranking the unassigned obj ect store operations in a list according to the order of the corresponding object store operations. 18. The system of claim 17 further comprising: means for selecting a first-ranked obj ect store operation in the list; means for identifying an obj ect affected by the first-ranked object store operation; means for determining whether the identified obj ect already has an existing obj ect store operations; means for determining whether the identified obj ect is detected in two or more existent entries, in response to determining that the identified obj ect already has existing object store operations; means for merging the two or more existent entries into a new dependency tree and adding the merged dependency tree as an entry to the first queue, in response to determining that the identified object is detected in the two or more existent entries,; means for adding the first-ranked object store operation to the merged dependency tree; and means for updating the list of the unassigned object store operations by removing the first-ranked object store operation from the list. 19. The system of claim 15 further comprising: means for determining whether there are a plurality of entries in the second queue; and means for executing entries in the second queue in parallel, in response to determining that there is an entry in the second queue. 20. The system of claim 19 further comprising: means for determining whether an entry in the first queue has a first dependency tree in the second queue; and means for sequentially executing obj ect store operations in the first dependency tree in a general left-to-right order responsive to the entry in the first queue having the first dependency tree in the second queue.
